summary: Add rows.Err() inspection after every for rows.Next() loop in the 9 novel commands so mid-iteration SQLite errors fail loudly instead of returning truncated results with exit 0.
reason: Greptile P2 (initial wave) and P1 (companies_profile.go follow-up) findings on PR #694: without rows.Err() the loop exit is indistinguishable from successful completion when SQLite interrupts (corrupted WAL page, storage error, context cancellation), and commands return partial results with no signal.
validated_outcome: go build ./... still passes; eleven rows.Err() checks present across the nine files (whats_new.go has two loops; companies_profile.go has two helper functions).
